operating_state: Net sales increased 23% to $8.1 billion driven by broad-based equipment demand recovery from COVID impacts, favorable global market conditions, and 6% price realization across segments. Equipment operations generated $1.0 billion in net income compared to $380 million in the prior year period. Financial services revenues declined 5% primarily due to lower finance income.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved significantly as cost of sales increased only 14% while net sales rose 23%, with cost of sales ratio improving to 72.1% from 77.7% due to price realization and operational leverage. Research and development expenses declined 14% and selling, administrative and general expenses decreased 5%, reflecting cost discipline and elimination of prior year voluntary employee-separation program costs.
material_events: The company adopted new credit loss accounting standards resulting in a $35 million reduction to retained earnings. Share repurchases totaled $352 million during the quarter with quarterly dividend declared at $0.90 per share. The company eliminated the one-month reporting lag for Wirtgen Group operations and implemented new operating segments beginning fiscal 2021, dividing agriculture and turf into production and precision agriculture and small agriculture and turf segments.
macro_exposures: COVID pandemic effects continue affecting operations with ongoing supply chain disruptions and potential supplier issues, prompting broadened supply base and increased inventory levels. The company provided short-term payment relief to dealers and retail customers affected by COVID impacts, with financing receivables granted relief representing approximately 4% of the portfolio. Currency translation adjustments contributed $396 million positive impact to comprehensive income.
forward_outlook: Industry sales forecasts show agricultural machinery growth across all regions with double-digit increases in North America and South America, while Europe shows mid-single-digit growth for 2021. Construction industry sales expected to increase with compact construction showing double-digit growth, while turf and utility equipment forecast up in North America. Management anticipates contributing to pension and OPEB plans during the remainder of fiscal 2021, including a voluntary contribution to a U.S. OPEB plan in the fourth quarter.
uncertainty_profile: Predicting financial performance remains subject to many assumptions due to unknown future financial effects of COVID including potential resurgences, supply chain disruptions, and customer impact on confidence levels and credit applications. Unrecognized tax benefits increased to $710 million from $668 million, with potential effects on the effective tax rate if recognized.
